title: Count Primes (Sieve of Eratosthenes)
description:
Return the number of primes strictly less than n.

**The point**: a per-number primality check is O(n · √n). The Sieve of Eratosthenes precomputes all primes up to n in **O(n log log n)** — almost linear, and a beautiful demonstration of clever marking-based algorithms.
hints: Naive: for every i from 2 to n-1, check primality. Each check is O(√i). Total O(n · √n) — too slow for big n.
The Sieve idea: instead of checking each number, MARK the composites as you find them.
When you find a prime p, mark p², p²+p, p²+2p, … (smaller multiples are already marked by smaller primes).
